_comment: Collective Investment Trust (CIT) substitution map. CITs are bank-administered trusts (not '40 Act funds) and don't file N-PORT, so their holdings are opaque. For index-tracking CITs, substituting a public mutual fund or ETF with the same index gives ~99% fidelity for X-ray purposes. The frontend should surface the substitution to the user before computing the X-ray. Patterns are matched case-insensitively against the user-entered fund name (substring match). Fidelity: 'high' = same index, expect <1% holdings drift; 'medium' = similar strategy, expect 5-15% drift; 'low' = rough analogue only.

substitutions.note: Both track the S&P 500 directly. The non-lending CIT forgoes securities lending income for a slight tracking-error advantage. VOO is the lowest-cost retail equivalent and shares series_id S000002839 with VFIAX (Admiral) and VFINX (Investor) — all three share classes resolve to the same holdings snapshot.

patterns: State Street US Bond Index Non-Lending
substitutions.note: State Street's CIT tracks Bloomberg US Aggregate (non-float-adjusted); BND tracks the float-adjusted variant. Holdings differ by <1% (float adjustment excludes bonds held by central banks). BND is already tracked in config/funds.json so its snapshot is available immediately. For an exact-index match, AGG (iShares Core US Aggregate Bond) is the better substitute but not currently ingested.
